resources.description: Bulk look up multiple Twitter/X tweets by their numeric tweet IDs in a single request. Each tweet includes full text, author profile, and engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes). Query param: ids (string, required) - comma-separated numeric tweet IDs, max 50, e.g. GET /tweets?ids=1110302988,1234567890. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Bulk look up multiple Twitter/X tweets by their numeric tweet IDs in a single request. Each tweet includes full text, author profile, and engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes). Query param: ids (string, required) - comma-separated numeric tweet IDs, max 50, e.g. GET /tweets?ids=1110302988,1234567890. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Look up a single Twitter/X tweet by its numeric tweet ID. Returns full tweet data including text, author profile, creation date, engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks), entities (hashtags, mentions, URLs), and referenced tweets (replies, quotes, retweets). Path param: id (string, required) - numeric tweet ID, e.g. GET /tweets/1110302988. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Look up a single Twitter/X tweet by its numeric tweet ID. Returns full tweet data including text, author profile, creation date, engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks), entities (hashtags, mentions, URLs), and referenced tweets (replies, quotes, retweets). Path param: id (string, required) - numeric tweet ID, e.g. GET /tweets/1110302988. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Search Twitter/X tweets with advanced filters. Returns up to 20 tweets per page, most recent first, with pagination. At least one filter required. Params: words, phrase, anyWords, noneWords, hashtags, from, to, mentioning, minReplies, minLikes, minReposts, since (YYYY-MM-DD), until, next_token. Each tweet includes text, author, and metrics. e.g. GET /tweets/search?words=bitcoin&from=elonmusk&minLikes=100. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Search Twitter/X tweets with advanced filters. Returns up to 20 tweets per page, most recent first, with pagination. At least one filter required. Params: words, phrase, anyWords, noneWords, hashtags, from, to, mentioning, minReplies, minLikes, minReposts, since (YYYY-MM-DD), until, next_token. Each tweet includes text, author, and metrics. e.g. GET /tweets/search?words=bitcoin&from=elonmusk&minLikes=100. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Get the latest tweets (timeline) from a specific Twitter/X user by their username. Returns up to 20 tweets per page sorted by most recent first, with pagination support. Each tweet includes text, metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes), and metadata. Query params: username (string, required) - Twitter screen name; next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response, e.g. GET /tweets/user?username=vitalik. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Get the latest tweets (timeline) from a specific Twitter/X user by their username. Returns up to 20 tweets per page sorted by most recent first, with pagination support. Each tweet includes text, metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes), and metadata. Query params: username (string, required) - Twitter screen name; next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response, e.g. GET /tweets/user?username=vitalik. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Get the list of Twitter/X users that a specific user follows, identified by their numeric user ID. Returns up to 50 following profiles per page with pagination support. Each account includes full profile data. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397/following. Query param: next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Get the list of Twitter/X users that a specific user follows, identified by their numeric user ID. Returns up to 50 following profiles per page with pagination support. Each account includes full profile data. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397/following. Query param: next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Get the list of Twitter/X users who follow a specific user, identified by their numeric user ID. Returns up to 50 follower profiles per page with pagination support. Each follower includes full profile data. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397/followers. Query param: next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Get the list of Twitter/X users who follow a specific user, identified by their numeric user ID. Returns up to 50 follower profiles per page with pagination support. Each follower includes full profile data. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397/followers. Query param: next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Bulk look up multiple Twitter/X user profiles by their numeric user IDs in a single request. Returns an array of full user profiles. Efficient for resolving many user IDs at once. Query param: ids (string, required) - comma-separated numeric Twitter user IDs, max 50, e.g. GET /users?ids=44196397,12,783214. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Bulk look up multiple Twitter/X user profiles by their numeric user IDs in a single request. Returns an array of full user profiles. Efficient for resolving many user IDs at once. Query param: ids (string, required) - comma-separated numeric Twitter user IDs, max 50, e.g. GET /users?ids=44196397,12,783214. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Look up a single Twitter/X user profile by their numeric user ID. Returns full profile data including id, name, username, bio, follower count, following count, tweet count, verification status, profile image URL, and pinned tweet id. Use this when you know the user ID. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Look up a single Twitter/X user profile by their numeric user ID. Returns full profile data including id, name, username, bio, follower count, following count, tweet count, verification status, profile image URL, and pinned tweet id. Use this when you know the user ID. Path param: id (string, required) - numeric Twitter user ID, e.g. GET /users/44196397. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Search Twitter/X for user accounts matching a keyword or phrase. Returns up to 20 matching user profiles per page with pagination support. Useful for discovering users by topic, name, or keyword. Query params: query (string, required) - search keyword or phrase; next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response, e.g. GET /users/search?query=bitcoin&next_token=xxx. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Search Twitter/X for user accounts matching a keyword or phrase. Returns up to 20 matching user profiles per page with pagination support. Useful for discovering users by topic, name, or keyword. Query params: query (string, required) - search keyword or phrase; next_token (string, optional) - pagination cursor from previous response, e.g. GET /users/search?query=bitcoin&next_token=xxx. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Look up a Twitter/X user profile by their screen name (username). Returns full profile data including id, name, username, bio, follower count, following count, tweet count, verification status, profile image URL, and pinned tweet id. Use this when you know the username (e.g. "vitalik", "elonmusk"). Path param: username (string, required) - the Twitter screen name, e.g. GET /users/by/username/vitalik. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

accepts.description: Look up a Twitter/X user profile by their screen name (username). Returns full profile data including id, name, username, bio, follower count, following count, tweet count, verification status, profile image URL, and pinned tweet id. Use this when you know the username (e.g. "vitalik", "elonmusk"). Path param: username (string, required) - the Twitter screen name, e.g. GET /users/by/username/vitalik. Returns X v2 API-compatible JSON.

resources.description: Base coin price mini-endpoint for major tokens only. Returns spot prices and 24h stats for well-known tickers like btc, solana, eth, xmr, ltc and other large-cap coins, never microcaps. Pass an optional ticker to get a single-coin 24h snapshot with price, symbol, changeRate, changePrice, high and low, or omit ticker to receive a compact basket of top tokens in a fixed order. Cost: 0.001 USDC per request.

accepts.description: Base coin price mini-endpoint for major tokens only. Returns spot prices and 24h stats for well-known tickers like btc, solana, eth, xmr, ltc and other large-cap coins, never microcaps. Pass an optional ticker to get a single-coin 24h snapshot with price, symbol, changeRate, changePrice, high and low, or omit ticker to receive a compact basket of top tokens in a fixed order. Cost: 0.001 USDC per request.

ticker.description: Optional ticker symbol for a single major token (case-insensitive). Examples: btc, solana, eth, xmr, ltc. When omitted the endpoint returns a compact basket of top tokens instead.

changePrice.description: Absolute 24h price change over the recent period for the asset (single mode only).

changeRate.description: Relative 24h change rate over the recent period for the asset (single mode only).

high.description: 24h high price for the asset (single mode only).

low.description: 24h low price for the asset (single mode only).

mode.description: Result mode: 'single' when ticker is provided, 'top' when ticker is omitted.

price.description: Spot price for the asset, using the upstream best bid/last price in a stable quote asset.

prices.description: For mode='top': ordered array of { ticker, symbol, price } for a fixed basket of major tokens, in the order: btc, solana, eth, xmr, bnb, xrp, trx, doge, ada, bch, hype, link, leo, xlm, zec, ltc, avax, hbar, dai, shib, ton, mnt, wlfi, cro.

symbol.description: Upstream trading symbol for the asset (e.g. BTC-USDT for single mode, or base asset symbol like BTC in the top basket).

ticker.description: Normalized lower-case ticker that was resolved (single mode only).

resources.description:
Returns current token prices with FDV, liquidity, and 24h price change, quoted in **USD**, **ETH**, or **BTC**.

### Rules
- Provide **either** `symbols` or `addresses`, not both.
- Max **30 entries** for either parameter (pipe-delimited).
- `quote_symbol` must be one of: `USD`, `ETH`, `BTC`.

accepts.description:
Returns current token prices with FDV, liquidity, and 24h price change, quoted in **USD**, **ETH**, or **BTC**.

### Rules
- Provide **either** `symbols` or `addresses`, not both.
- Max **30 entries** for either parameter (pipe-delimited).
- `quote_symbol` must be one of: `USD`, `ETH`, `BTC`.

addresses.description: Pipe-delimited list of token contract addresses (e.g., 0xabc...|0xdef...). Cannot be used with 'symbols'. Max 30 addresses.

estimate_cost.description: When true, only returns the SLAM token cost for this request. Does not include token price data.

quote_symbol.description: Quote token or currency to return prices in.

symbols.description: Pipe-delimited list of token symbols to query (e.g., SWARM|VADER). Cannot be used with 'addresses'. Max 30 symbols.

block.description: The block number for this price snapshot.

quote.description: Quote token or currency (USD, ETH, BTC).

slam.description: The SLAM token cost for this API call.

resources.description:
Returns trending tokens across the entire blockchain for a specified activity window and optional category.
The size of the increase in active wallets over the activity window determines the token's trending rank.

accepts.description:
Returns trending tokens across the entire blockchain for a specified activity window and optional category.
The size of the increase in active wallets over the activity window determines the token's trending rank.

activity_window.description: The length of time over which activity is measured.

category.description: Filters to a specific subset of tokens. Currently only 'virtuals' is supported on Base.

estimate_cost.description: When true, returns only the SLAM token cost estimation.

num.description: The maximum number of tokens to return. Maximum: 250.

block.description: The block number at which this data snapshot was generated

chain.description: The name of the blockchain network (e.g., 'base')

slam.description: The SLAM token cost for this API call

tokens.description: List of trending tokens for the specified activity window and category

fdv.description: The token’s fully diluted valuation in USD

block.description: The block number of the first mint

hash.description: The transaction hash of the first mint

utc.description: The UTC timestamp of the mint (ISO 8601 format)

liquidity.description: The liquidity value for the token in USD

name.description: The full name of the token

rank.description: The token’s trending rank, determined by change in active wallets.

cur.description: The current token price in USD

utc.description: The UTC timestamp of the block used for the response (ISO 8601 format)

resources.description: Base text-to-speech API paid via x402. Converts text input into a hosted audio URL. Dynamic pricing for TTS: the prepaid price is computed per request based on the input and charged upfront via x402. Prepaid price for pure discovery requests without text is an upper bound; clients should re-run discovery with their concrete input if they need an exact quote.

accepts.description: Base text-to-speech API paid via x402. Converts text input into a hosted audio URL. Dynamic pricing for TTS: the prepaid price is computed per request based on the input and charged upfront via x402. Prepaid price for pure discovery requests without text is an upper bound; clients should re-run discovery with their concrete input if they need an exact quote.

action.description: Optional action. Omit for TTS. Use 'list' to list available voices instead of generating speech.

text.description: Text to convert to speech when action is omitted (TTS mode). Required for paid TTS calls.

voice_id.description: Optional ElevenLabs voice_id. If omitted, a default narrator voice is used.

action.description: Action performed: 'tts' for text-to-speech, 'list' for voice listing.

chars.description: Number of characters used for pricing in TTS calls.

price_usdc.description: Price charged for this call in USDC (6 decimals, rounded up). Minimum is 0.001 USDC.

tts_url.description: Public URL of the generated audio file hosted on ImageKit (TTS only).

voice_id.description: Voice ID used for the TTS call (TTS only).

voices.description: For action=list: available voices with trimmed metadata (voice_id, name, accent, descriptive, age, language, use_case).

resources.name: CDP Facilitator
resources.description: Best-in-class x402 facilitator. Fee-free USDC settlement on Base Mainnet. KYT/OFAC checks on every transaction.

resources.name: Corbits
resources.description: A production grade facilitator which supports multi-network, multi-token, multi-payment schemes.

resources.name: Mogami facilitator
resources.description: A free, developer-focused, production-ready facilitator for x402 payments. Use it instantly online, or launch your own Docker image in seconds.

resources.name: OpenX402.ai Facilitator
resources.description: The first permissionless, gasless and omnichain x402 facilitator that any x402 server can use without login

resources.name: PayAI Facilitator
resources.description: Accept x402 payments on all networks including Avalanche, Base, Polygon, Sei, Solana, and more! Get started in less than 1 minute. Supports all tokens. No API Keys required.

resources.name: Treasure Facilitator
resources.description: Treasure x402 Facilitator available on Base and Base Sepolia. Supports EIP-3009 compliant tokens including USDC, MAGIC, SMOL, MIO, AI Frens tokens, and more.

resources.name: WorldFun Facilitator
resources.description: AWE World.fun’s x402 Facilitator enables fee-free EIP-3009 payments in USDC and other ERC-20 tokens, handling verification and settlement on Base so developers don’t need blockchain infrastructure.

resources.name: x402.org Facilitator
resources.description: Default testnet facilitator for x402

resources.name: x402.rs Facilitator
resources.description: Independent, open-source facilitator in Rust. Easy to self-host, extend to new networks, or use via our hosted instance.
